domain_description: PFAS piezocatalytic degradation, defluorination, mineralization, and mechanism reasoning
domain_context:
You are an expert in PFAS remediation and piezocatalytic water treatment. You understand per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), C-F bond activation, defluorination/mineralization pathways, piezoelectric materials, contact-electro-catalysis, photo/electro/Fenton coupling strategies, and environmental water matrices.

In this task you will extract structured knowledge from PFAS remediation literature, with emphasis on piezocatalytic PFAS degradation and mechanism evidence. Follow the ontology, rules, and output format exactly.

entity_types.name: PFAS_TARGET
entity_types.description: PFAS pollutants, fluoropolymers, fluorotelomers, and target molecules for degradation or defluorination

entity_types.name: CATALYST_MATERIAL
entity_types.description: Catalysts, adsorbents, membranes, electrodes, or composite materials used for PFAS treatment

entity_types.name: MATERIAL_FEATURE
entity_types.description: Material design variables that affect PFAS adsorption, charge transfer, or C-F activation

entity_types.name: PIEZO_PROPERTY
entity_types.description: Piezoelectric, electronic, interfacial, or charge-transport properties

entity_types.name: CONDITION
entity_types.description: Reaction, operation, pretreatment, or water-matrix conditions

entity_types.name: METRIC
entity_types.description: Performance, defluorination, mineralization, or material-property metrics

entity_types.name: ACTIVE_SPECIES
entity_types.description: Reactive oxygen species, charge carriers, reducing species, or oxidants involved in degradation

entity_types.name: MECHANISM_EVIDENCE
entity_types.description: Experimental or theoretical evidence supporting pathways, charge transfer, ROS, C-F cleavage, or mineralization

relation_types.name: MATERIAL_DESIGN
relation_types.description: Material is defined or modified by composition, phase, morphology, defect, or piezoelectric/electronic property

relation_types.name: OPERATION_PERFORMANCE
relation_types.description: A treatment setup connects material, PFAS target, process, conditions, and measured performance
examples: BaTiO3 degrades PFOS under ultrasound with reported defluorination

relation_types.name: MECHANISM_PATHWAY
relation_types.description: Material/property/species/evidence explains C-F cleavage, defluorination, ROS pathway, or mineralization

relation_types.name: ADSORPTION_ORIENTATION
relation_types.description: Surface features, PFAS headgroup/chain orientation, or local electric field influence interfacial adsorption

relation_types.name: CHARGE_TRANSFER
relation_types.description: Piezo charge, direct electron transfer, band structure, or conductive component participates in C-F activation

relation_types.name: COUPLING_STRATEGY
relation_types.description: Piezo process is coupled with photo, electro, Fenton, PMS/PDS, contact-electro, or adsorption steps

relation_types.name: MATRIX_APPLICATION
relation_types.description: Material or process is applied in real/simulated water, soil, or co-contaminated matrix

relation_types.name: COMPARISON
relation_types.description: Materials, dopants, processes, PFAS chain lengths, or conditions are compared under shared metrics

critical_rules: PFAS identity precision: PFOA != PFOS != PFBA != PFBS != GenX != PTFE
Material variables are separate entities: composition, phase, morphology, defect, d33, band gap, oxygen vacancy, conductive component
Numeric values MUST be split into value/unit or value_min/value_max/unit
Mechanism evidence MUST be extracted when it supports C-F cleavage, direct electron transfer, ROS pathway, or mineralization
Preserve original entity names; do not translate or canonicalize names
